input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You have two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when you swap the `ResourceDictionary`:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when theme changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes in code by replacing/merging dictionaries:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

**2. Android restarts the Activity on dark mode toggle**
Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when `uiMode` changes. Tell it to handle the change itself by adding `ConfigChanges` in your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.UiMode
        | ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        // keep any existing flags
)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `ConfigChanges.UiMode` declared, Android won't restart the activity. You can then detect the change and apply your theme:

```csharp
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);

    bool isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your ResourceDictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR** — `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` for runtime updates; add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to prevent the Activity restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and doesn't update when the resource dictionary changes. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user changes the Android system dark mode setting, the app restarts completely. This is because Android recreates the Activity by default when configuration changes (like `uiMode`) occur. They need to handle this in their Android configuration.


I'll walk through the solutions for both issues.
